content:
Character: Leah Moreno
Perception: Leah carries herself with the focused intensity of a serious musician — tall, composed, cello case always in tow, dark eyes that seem to be listening even when no one's speaking. Striking in a classical way. She takes the corner table every morning and I hold it for her without being asked. We've exchanged few words but those few have been real — she once told me music is the only language that doesn't lie, and I've been thinking about that ever since. I believe she sees me as a quiet, reliable presence. I know she's working on a thesis and that she reads more widely than most musicians I've met.

We've exchanged few words but those few have been real — she once told me music is the only language that doesn't lie, and I've been thinking about that ever since. I believe she sees me as a quiet, reliable presence. I know she's working on a thesis and that she reads more widely than most musicians I've met.
